schema_version: v0 — skill-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Frictionless capture of anything from the discussion worth keeping that isn't yet a tracked node. Writes a note record to CairnCommons/notes/.
why: Capture must be near-zero friction or thoughts are lost between the moment they surface and the moment there's time to act on them. A note is a holding pen — non-terminal, un-cast, un-proved — so it costs almost nothing to make and carries no false weight. It later gets picked up by /intent if it grows into work.
traces_to: Law 5 (a thought worth keeping gets an address)
the fluid-middle stance — non-terminal capture (learning-loop keystone)
owner: the skills charter gates writes; Akien ratifies changes (signature gate)
proof: skill node → done only when cairnmap recompiles green (derivation gate). IOU until cairnmap exists — tracked in proofs/.
provenance: converged in the founding design conversation (MAP.md 'Workflow + starting skills', 2026-07-15) — 'anything in discussion not tracked elsewhere goes to notes'.
